title: King Narai | Maritime Asia
content: continental trading powers king narai (r. 1656-88) influential ayutthayan ruler remained power three decades. highly active figure global diplomacy, narai welcomed multiple foreign embassies including ambassador zheng chenggong (koxinga) 1662. siam became site ferocious competition deerskin trade. contrast parts southeast asia struggle two foreign parties, king narai attempted secure profits lucrative deerskin trade sending royal junks manned chinese sailors directly japan. result conflict dutch east india company, resisted move. 1663, voc decided blockade coast secure hold trade. 1664, long-distance commerce standstill two richly laden siamese ships company hands, court retreated, confirming dutch control deerskin trade via treaty. despite treaty, narai continued dispatch ships japan. late 1660s, king back deerskin business, leaving voc little recourse unless wanted stage another expensive blockade.

title: Pirates, States, and Diplomacy in a Multipolar Maritime Asia  | Maritime Asia
content: classroom simulation exercise page intended brief introduction multi-part teaching exercise designed adam clulow xing hang use classroom. exercise simulates complex, multipolar maritime world east southeast asia second half seventeenth century. students take separate roles representatives armed maritime powers like zheng dutch east india company, continental agrarian powers like tokugawa japan qing china continental trading powers like ayutthaya (siam). exercise split three parts ideally divided across number classes. stage, students presented series objectives priorities. negotiate groups students representing rival powers using combination diplomatic letters embassies. many students, exercise whole may remind mock united nations conventions might experienced exercise available request instructor. found generates fast-paced highly immersive educational experience frequently high point students semester. end exercise, students learn following: second half seventeenth century singular moment east southeast asian history two armed enterprises, one european, one asian, dominated sea lanes, forcing range different powers respond. maritime powers dominated waves, seventeenth-century east southeast asia multipolar environment characterized multiple political permutations. generated complex sometimes chaotic international environment different powers interacted, opposed allied qing might ally voc, voc siam, exercise designed simulate multiple permutations. asian markets represented greatest commercial prize seventeenth century. much scholarly work focused china seemingly insatiable demand silver, japan huge market right. particular, japan’s sustained demand deerskins generated fierce commercial competition. voc zheng organization hybrid enterprises possessed essentially sovereign powers. made ready use maritime violence, described variously legal privateering illegal piracy depending viewpoint. period, distinction legality illegality, legitimate state armed pirate enterprise complex subject constantly change. zheng organization formidable competitor voc european powers. organizations represents alternative path global history asian maritime enterprises might emerged challenge european equivalents. nothing inevitable rise europe. voc powerful european overseas enterprises seventeenth century, lurched setback setback. overview exercise early 1670s, representatives five key powers (factions) east southeast asia gathered undisclosed location discuss resolve escalating conflict across region. exercise divided three stages ideally split across three separate classes. factions follows: zheng maritime network dutch east india company (voc) ayutthaya (siam) tokugawa japan qing china note: dutch east india company referred vereenigde oost-indische compagnie dutch. commonly referred acronym, voc. kingdom ayutthaya frequently known siam roughly corresponds modern day thailand. powers divided three broad groups factions. strengths weaknesses. armed maritime powers: zheng voc strengths: formidable maritime trading powers, blockade coast territorial powers attack shipping weaknesses: hybrid organizations conventional standing, lack legitimacy. zheng claim rights rule china, engaged struggle qing control mainland. continental agrarian powers qing china tokugawa japan strengths: wealthy markets trading powers want access high prestige east asian superpowers weaknesses: huge armies limited navies. vulnerable maritime blockades. qing disadvantage zheng claim legitimacy china’s rightful rulers continental trading powers ayutthaya (siam) advantages: flexible polity heavily invested trade, willing make compromises, open trade weaknesses: limited navy. maritime blockades potentially devastate coast. heavily reliant long-distance trade profits flowing directly court. students strong preference joining particular group, may select preferred group sign-up sheet. group membership determined first-come, first-served basis. choose sign assigned group. works three stages rounds. end round, series votes indicate state negotiations. emphasis throughout exercise historically grounded negotiations diplomacy. readings
